content: HIV diagnosis follows CDC algorithm: 1) Fourth-generation HIV-1/2 antigen/antibody combination immunoassay (detects p24 antigen + antibodies, window period ~18 days); 2) If reactive, HIV-1/HIV-2 antibody differentiation assay confirms type; 3) If differentiation assay negative/indeterminate, HIV-1 RNA NAT confirms acute infection. Rapid tests provide results in <30 minutes but require confirmation. Viral load quantifies HIV RNA for monitoring treatment response. CD4 count assesses immune status: normal 500-1,500 cells/μL; AIDS defined as CD4 <200 cells/μL or CD4 percentage <14%. Baseline resistance testing (genotype) guides regimen selection. Screening recommended for all adults 15-65 years and high-risk populations.
